item.title: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Reliance is weaving AI into telecom services used by more than 500 million people.

item.title: The CEO of Allbirds’ new AI biz has a plan, but no employees
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Call it a startup with a sole founder and a very large seed round, but what's next is less clear.

item.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China. ASML says it isn’t
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

item.title: Telegram ban in India sparks a rush to VPNs, rival apps
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: Telegram argues India should block specific content, not an entire platform used by millions.

item.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed DeductiveAI for up to $85M
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: DeductiveAI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.

item.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.

item.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.

item.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

item.title: The 11 standout startups from YC’s Demo Day, according to VCs
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin, Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: TechCrunch spoke to investors to find the hottest startups in the Spring 2026 YC batch. Some of them commanded valuations of over $175 million, VCs said.

item.title: Rivian owners file lawsuit alleging false promises on self-driving features
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Plaintiffs in the class -action complaint allege Rivian falsely promised for years it would bring hands-free driving to its first-generation R1 vehicles.

item.title: Almost half of US singles feel negatively about AI in dating, Match says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: About 47% of singles look negatively at the use of AI in dating -- but many dating app users are open to AI helping with profile punch-ups and conversation starters.

item.title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy has said this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

item.title: Mivo’s new app takes a mindful approach to managing screen time
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Notably, unlike other apps that might just try to pull you away from your phone, Mivo lets the user decide if they want to continue, encouraging users to become more aware of how and why they’re using it instead.

item.title: AI data centers just got a government-mandated fast lane to the grid
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: FERC told grid operators to give data centers a fast lane for interconnections, but it failed to address electricity supply shortages.

item.title: The smartphone era created an attention crisis — slow tech is fixing it
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”

item.title: Texas government data breach allowed hackers to steal 3 million driver’s licenses and passports
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: A data breach involving government-issued ID documents affects over 3 million people in Texas.

item.title: ‘Queer Eye’ life coach Karamo Brown launches Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: After spending a year and a half focusing on his own journey — from fitness and nutrition to meditation, sobriety, relationships, and personal growth — Brown wants to help others do the same.

item.title: Apple opens up App Store to new competition in Brazil
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Apple’s grip on iPhone app distribution is loosening in another major market: Brazil.

item.title: MapTap, a daily geography game, is my new Wordle
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: MapTap is a phone game that will make you feel smarter after you play it.

item.title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The startup trains embodied AI and world models using Medal’s dataset of 2 billion videos per year from 10 million monthly active users.
